title: Blue Origin continues work on lunar landers during recovery from New Glenn explosion
summary: Blue Origin is advancing its Blue Moon lunar landers with seven vehicles in production despite recovering from the recent New Glenn rocket explosion. The company remains focused on lunar lander development amid the incident.

title: Iridium folds Aireon aviation safety service into Rocket Lab-bound business
summary: Iridium Communications has fully acquired Aireon, integrating its aircraft-tracking service ahead of a planned $8 billion sale to Rocket Lab. The move consolidates Iridium's aviation safety operations under its Rocket Lab-bound business.

title: NASA adds three European firms to the commercial data program
summary: NASA's Commercial Satellite Data Acquisition program (CSDA) awarded contracts to eight new commercial satellite data providers, including three European firms: Kuva Space (Finland) and OroraTech. This expansion aims to enhance NASA's access to diverse commercial satellite data.

title: The term ‘dual-use’ is misleading and dangerous
summary: The article argues that the term 'dual-use' in space technology is misleading, as technology's purpose depends on intent and context rather than inherent characteristics. It emphasizes that the focus should be on how technology is employed politically and strategically.

title: BAE microchip designed for strategic defense missions passes radiation test
summary: BAE Systems' Endura microchip has successfully passed radiation tests, making it suitable for classified and missile-defense satellite programs. The company is positioning the chip for strategic defense missions.

title: Connected Aircraft Fleet Set to Exceed 70,000 by 2035
summary: The In-Flight Connectivity (IFC) market is projected to see long-term growth, with the number of connected aircraft exceeding 70,000 by 2035. IFC penetration is also expected to increase from its current 48%.

title: Centre for Space Futures, Novaspace and SpaceTech Gulf  Sign Agreement to Develop Global Space Capability Mapping Dashboard
summary: The Centre for Space Futures, Novaspace, and SpaceTech Gulf have signed an agreement to develop a global space capability mapping dashboard. This collaboration aims to enhance international space capabilities through a shared digital platform.

title: Tianwen-2 arrives at asteroid Kamo’oalewa, first image revealed
summary: China's Tianwen-2 spacecraft has reached the near-Earth asteroid Kamoʻoalewa, capturing its first image which reveals a small, elongated rocky body. The mission aims to return samples from the asteroid.

title: NASA and Small Business Administration partner on funding key space technologies
summary: NASA and the U.S. Small Business Administration (SBA) are collaborating to secure funding for companies developing essential space technologies. The partnership aims to attract investment in critical space components.

title: Airbus to build Aeolus-2 wind-monitoring satellite
summary: The European Space Agency has selected Airbus Defence and Space to develop the Aeolus-2 satellite, which will succeed the original Aeolus wind-monitoring mission. Airbus will lead the construction of this new satellite.

title: Even astronauts in space saw America 250 fireworks on the Fourth of July. See their ISS view of Los Angeles (video)
summary: Astronauts aboard the ISS witnessed America's 250th Independence Day fireworks, sharing a video of Los Angeles' celebrations. The U.S. marked its 250th anniversary with widespread festivities.

title: Watch SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket launch 81 satellites early on July 7
summary: A SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket is set to launch 81 satellites to orbit from California on July 7, with the event available for live viewing.

title: More clues surface about the origins of interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS
summary: Observations of interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS by the Very Large Telescope in Chile align with data from the James Webb Space Telescope (JWST), providing further clues about its origins. The comet's fast-moving trajectory suggests it originated outside our solar system.

title: NASA just found a planet 'hiding' in TESS spacecraft data, all thanks to Einstein
summary: NASA's TESS spacecraft discovered a new exoplanet using a method inspired by Einstein's theories. The planet was detected by analyzing data from the spacecraft's observations.

title: Black holes buried in mysterious 'little red dot' galaxies could blast cosmic ghosts at Earth
summary: The James Webb Space Telescope discovered mysterious 'little red dot' galaxies in the early universe that may contain buried black holes. These black holes could potentially fire high-energy neutrinos toward Earth.

title: Unidentified metal spheres found on Australian beach are 'debris from a foreign rocket body', space agency says
summary: Unidentified metal spheres found on an Australian beach were identified as debris from a foreign rocket body by the Australian Space Agency. Residents in Queensland have been warned to be cautious of the potential space debris.

title: 'Acceleration without fuel:' Revolutionary superconducting thruster harnesses Earth's magnetic field in 1st orbital test
summary: A novel space thruster using superconducting magnets has completed its first orbital tests. The technology could enable spacecraft propulsion using only solar power by harnessing Earth's magnetic field.

title: Japan's Hayabusa2 probe captures remarkable photo of a two-headed asteroid 62 million miles away
summary: Japan's Hayabusa2 probe captured a remarkable photo of the double-lobed asteroid Torifune, located 62 million miles away, during a close approach on July 5.

title: Artemis moon astronauts visit Capitol Hill | Space photo of the day for July 6, 2026
summary: NASA's Artemis II astronauts visited Capitol Hill after completing a 10-day journey around the moon and back in April 2026. The visit highlights their mission and engagement with policymakers.

title: NASA will have to find a way to service its new alien-hunting space telescope
summary: NASA is exploring robotic servicing options for its upcoming Habitable Worlds Observatory, a space telescope designed to search for alien life. This could involve autonomous robots performing maintenance and upgrades in space.

title: NASA Takes Flight For America’s 250th
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman leads a flyover in a personally owned Northrop F-5 Tiger aircraft over the National Mall in Washington, D.C., as part of America's 250th anniversary celebrations. The event highlights the nation's history of pushing boundaries in exploration and spaceflight.

title: NASA Seeks Industry Input on Second Phase of Commercial Space Stations
summary: NASA released a draft Request for Proposals (RFP) seeking industry feedback on the next phase of its commercial space stations strategy to ensure a smooth transition of low Earth orbit activities from the ISS. The move reflects ongoing collaboration with American companies to maintain a continuous human presence in space.

title: NASA’s CAPSTONE Completes Extended Mission Testing Lunar Technologies
summary: NASA's CAPSTONE mission has completed an extended mission testing lunar technologies, advancing autonomous navigation and communication capabilities for future Moon missions. The spacecraft validated key technologies in lunar orbit, supporting NASA's goal of a sustained human presence on the Moon.

title: NASA Webb Uncovers Unusual Galaxy Shaped by Cosmic Collision
summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope reveals a transformed image of the galaxy Centaurus A, showcasing its complex structure hidden by dust in visible light. The new infrared observations highlight the galaxy's intricate details due to Webb's unprecedented sensitivity.

title: Examining Algal Blooms in Blue Mesa
summary: Researchers used satellite data to link harmful algal blooms in Blue Mesa Reservoir to warm water and low water levels. The study highlights environmental impacts on one of Colorado's largest reservoirs.

title: NASA’s Hubble Spies Stellar Sparkler for July 4th
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured a festive image of stars resembling a red, white, and blue sparkler. The image was released to celebrate the July 4th holiday.

title: NASA’s Hubble Captures Crimson Cloud Sparkling with White, Blue Stars
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of a crimson gas cloud illuminated by bright blue and white stars. The vibrant colors highlight the dynamic processes occurring in this stellar region.

title: NASA’s Hubble Spots Star-Spangled Cosmic Scene
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of the globular cluster Messier 3 (M3), showcasing over 500,000 stars in red, white, and blue hues. This cosmic scene highlights the vibrant and dense stellar population within M3.

title: NASA’s Artemis II Breaks Agency Streaming Record
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission achieved record-breaking viewership with over 149.4 million views across its live coverage, demonstrating strong global engagement. The 24/7 streams of the launch, lunar flyby, and splashdown highlighted sustained public interest in the mission.

title: Good Morning, Earth!
summary: NASA astronaut Chris Williams captured an orbital sunrise photo from the International Space Station on June 26, 2026. The ISS completes 16 orbits around Earth daily, experiencing 16 sunrises and sunsets.

title: Launch Preview: Long March 10B and Vikram-I debut
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled internationally this week, including the debuts of China's Long March 10B and India's Vikram-I rockets. Three Falcon 9 flights are also expected from SpaceX.

title: Vast’s Haven-1 continues to advance through milestones ahead of planned 2027 launch
summary: Vast Space's Haven-1 commercial space station is progressing toward its planned Q1 2027 launch, having achieved key milestones. The station aims to be the world's first commercial space station.

title: Starship in Florida pushing for launch this year
summary: SpaceX is accelerating preparations for Starship launches from Cape Canaveral, aiming for a launch this year. The company and its contractors are making progress toward this goal.

title: NASA announces new lander contracts for Moon Base Phase One
summary: NASA announced four new lunar lander contracts as part of its Moon Base Phase One initiative. The contracts aim to support future lunar exploration and base construction efforts.

title: Blue Origin accelerates LC-36 recovery via hybrid plan
summary: Blue Origin is accelerating the recovery of Launch Complex-36 at Cape Canaveral using a hybrid plan following a recent anomaly that damaged the facility during the New Glenn launch. The company aims to restore operations swiftly after the incident.

title: Launch Preview: Swift rescue mission, final Atlas satellite launch
summary: A rescue mission for the Swift observatory is planned alongside the final launch of an Atlas satellite. Multiple orbital launches are scheduled globally this week.

title: Pegasus XL set to air launch Swift Boost Mission to save NASA space telescope
summary: A Northrop Grumman Pegasus XL rocket is scheduled for its first mission since 2021 to air launch the Swift Boost Mission, aiming to save a NASA space telescope. This mission marks a significant effort to extend the operational life of the Swift Observatory.

title: Euclid studies Milky Way center, supports future Roman observations
summary: ESA's Euclid space telescope captured a new image of the Milky Way's center, providing data that will aid future observations by NASA's Roman Space Telescope. The image highlights Euclid's capability to study complex regions of the galaxy.

title: Ship 40 completes single-engine static fire; Booster 20 and Pad 2 see new work
summary: SpaceX conducted a single-engine static fire test for Ship 40. Booster 20 and Pad 2 also saw new work being done.

title: Space Shuttle Endeavour displayed vertically in new exhibit at California Science Center
summary: Space Shuttle Endeavour has been displayed vertically in a new exhibit at the California Science Center. The exhibit aims to educate visitors about the shuttle's history and contributions to space exploration.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The news source is a B2B website focused on the technology, engineering, and construction sectors, covering business-related content.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is hosting events to present the follow-up to the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025 (CM25), detailing planned activities and industrial opportunities across ESA programs.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new robotic arm designed for versatile applications in the space industry. This innovation aims to enhance robotic operations in space missions.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational and ready to resume formation flying, enabling artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are contributing to ESA's missions by testing and selecting materials that can withstand the harsh conditions of space. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section is leading this effort with innovative approaches.

title: Exclusive: Ravee Optics Raises $6M Seed to Build Compact Laser Terminals
summary: Ravee Optics has raised $6 million in a seed funding round to develop compact laser communication terminals. The company aims to make laser comms more accessible with lighter, smaller, and scalable solutions.

title: The Biggest Fundraises of H1 2026
summary: The article highlights the largest fundraises in the first half of 2026 for the space industry. It emphasizes how increased investment is enabling rocket launches to support various payloads globally.

title: Inside the Deepening Ties Between Europe and the Middle East
summary: Europe's strained geopolitical ties with the US have fostered stronger space collaboration with Middle Eastern companies. This shift highlights growing strategic partnerships in the space sector.

title: True Anomaly Approaches, Images Rocket Lab Craft
summary: True Anomaly successfully captured and delivered images of a Rocket Lab spacecraft within 61 hours of receiving the order, beating the 72-hour deadline. This demonstrates True Anomaly's rapid response capabilities in space imaging.

title: Exclusive: Austria Taps R-Space for Its Second Military Sat
summary: Austria has selected R-Space to develop its second military satellite, Aurora, as part of a series of government-backed orbital technology demonstrations. The mission aims to validate new technologies in orbit.

title: Exclusive: Lunar Forge Unveils Plans to Build Lunar Infrastructure Out of Dust
summary: Lunar Forge announced plans to construct lunar infrastructure using regolith to reduce costs for fission reactors. The approach aims to minimize expenses by utilizing lunar dust for structural support and shielding.

title: NASA Praises Blue Origin’s Mishap Progress, Announces Moon Base Contracts
summary: NASA commended Blue Origin's progress in addressing past failures and awarded contracts for moon base missions. The agency aims for monthly lunar landings using updated lander designs.

title: Payload Field Guide: CLPS Missions
summary: The article discusses CLPS (Commercial Lunar Payload Services) missions, focusing on lunar landers and their role in upcoming Moon missions. It highlights the progress and objectives of these missions as part of NASA's lunar exploration efforts.

title: Exclusive: Satellogic Expands AI Apps with SpaceKnow Partnership
summary: Satellogic partnered with SpaceKnow to develop AI applications using Satellogic's satellite imagery. This collaboration aims to enhance geospatial intelligence analysis capabilities.

title: Rocket Lab Enters the Satcom Market With $8B Iridium Deal
summary: Rocket Lab has entered the satellite communications market through an $8 billion deal with Iridium. This marks a significant expansion for the company into space applications.

title: An Expedition Across the Arctic-Space Connection With David Marsh
summary: David Marsh recently returned from an expedition to the Arctic to study parallels between Arctic and space environments. The findings are discussed in an interview on the 'On Orbit' program.

title: Neo Space and Hughes Deploy 3 Ground Gateways to Support IFC
summary: Neo Space Group and Hughes Network Systems have deployed three new Hughes ground gateways in Saudi Arabia and Europe to support NSG’s in-flight connectivity (IFC). This deployment aims to enhance NSG's satellite-based connectivity services for airlines.
company: Neo Space Group and Hughes Network Systems

title: Univity Signs MoU With Indonesia’s Telkomsat on VLEO and D2D Connectivity
summary: Univity has signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with Indonesia's Telkomsat to collaborate on satellite connectivity, including Very Low Earth Orbit (VLEO) architecture and direct-to-device (D2D) connectivity.

title: Mission to Rescue NASA Swift Observatory Launches
summary: A mission to rescue NASA's Swift Observatory has launched, with teams establishing contact with the Katalyst Space LINK servicing spacecraft. Northrop Grumman executed the launch.

title: Cyber in Space Signs Partnership Deal with ExeQuantum
summary: Cyber in Space has partnered with ExeQuantum to enhance cyber resilience and quantum-readiness services for the global aerospace and space ecosystem. The collaboration aims to deliver advanced security solutions to organizations in the industry.

title: Amazon Leo Constellation Ready to Support Initial Service After ULA Mission
summary: Amazon's Leo constellation has reached sufficient satellite deployment to enable initial service across key latitudes following a ULA launch. The milestone was achieved after a mission conducted in the early hours of Thursday.

title: Isar Aerospace Lands Big Win With Planet Launch Contract
summary: Isar Aerospace secured a launch contract with Planet to launch one of Planet's next-generation Pelican satellites, as early as late 2026. This marks a significant milestone for the German launcher currently in development.

title: Mitsubishi Electric to Acquire Infostellar
summary: Mitsubishi Electric has announced its intent to acquire Infostellar, a prominent company in the Japanese space sector. The acquisition aims to strengthen Mitsubishi Electric's position in the industry.

title: Vodafone Ireland Conducts Emergency Services D2D Call With AST SpaceMobile JV
summary: Vodafone Ireland successfully tested a direct-to-device (D2D) call with AST SpaceMobile's satellite constellation for emergency services connectivity. The test is part of efforts to enhance communication for first responders.

title: The Internet in Space: The Apps that Dominate Satellite Traffic and Why it Matters
summary: The article discusses the growing importance of satellite networks for non-government applications and the types of apps dominating satellite traffic as thousands of new satellites are launched into orbit. It explores the practical uses of satellite internet beyond traditional applications.
